message:
fix(scripts): bd1f35 thread-stress default target dir to home cache

bd1f35_thread_stress_run.sh defaulted FLC_BD1F35_CARGO_TARGET_DIR to
\`/data/tmp/cargo-target-bd1f35\`. That path works on the local dev
host but rch remote workers have /data/tmp restricted, producing
"Permission denied (os error 13) at path
/data/tmp/cargo-target-bd1f35VWAVQ0" when the workspace test sweep
(\`rch exec -- cargo test --workspace\`) tried to run the gate.

Default to \${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-${HOME}/.cache/frankenlibc/cargo-target-bd1f35}
so the workspace sweep picks up the upstream CARGO_TARGET_DIR (which
the rch invocation already sets to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc),
falling back to a per-user cache path that's reliably writable on
both local and remote workers. Explicit FLC_BD1F35_CARGO_TARGET_DIR
overrides take precedence for callers that need a specific layout.

Verified: cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test
bd1f35_thread_stress_artifacts_test passes locally after the change.

files.patch:
@@ -20,7 +20,11 @@ TIMEOUT_SECONDS="${TIMEOUT_SECONDS:-300}"
 STRESS_SEED="${FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STRESS_SEED:-4242}"
 FANOUT_ITERS="${FLC_BD1F35_FANOUT_ITERS:-3}"
 DETACH_JOIN_ITERS="${FLC_BD1F35_DETACH_JOIN_ITERS:-3}"
-THREAD_STRESS_TARGET_DIR="${FLC_BD1F35_CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-/data/tmp/cargo-target-bd1f35}"
+# Default the cargo target directory to a path under the user's home cache so
+# the gate works on rch remote workers where /data/tmp is not writable. Caller
+# can still override via FLC_BD1F35_CARGO_TARGET_DIR or by setting a per-host
+# CARGO_TARGET_DIR upstream of the script.
+THREAD_STRESS_TARGET_DIR="${FLC_BD1F35_CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-${HOME}/.cache/frankenlibc/cargo-target-bd1f35}}"
 
 if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   echo "FAIL: jq is required" >&2
